title: Extend the TikTok Deadline Act
stage_label: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation.
plain_summary: This bill gives TikTok more time to find a new owner before it gets banned in the United States. Right now, TikTok has 270 days to be sold to a new company. This bill would change that to 540 days, which is almost twice as long. The extra time would help TikTok find someone to buy the company so it can keep running in America.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Extend the TikTok Deadline Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Extension of Deadline
sections.text: This section changes an existing law called the Protecting Americans from Foreign Adversary Controlled Applications Act. The original law gave TikTok two hundred seventy days to be sold to avoid being banned in the United States. This new law changes that time limit to five hundred forty days instead. This means TikTok would have almost twice as long to find a buyer before facing a potential ban.
